examples.title: Requirements Traceability Coverage Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate and report requirements traceability coverage percentage
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT: requirementsList[], traceabilityMatrix[][]
 totalRequirements ← LENGTH(requirementsList)
 linkedCount ← 0
 
 FOR i FROM 0 TO totalRequirements-1 DO
 hasLink ← FALSE
 FOR j FROM 0 TO LENGTH(traceabilityMatrix[i])-1 DO
 IF traceabilityMatrix[i][j] IS NOT NULL THEN
 hasLink ← TRUE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 IF hasLink = TRUE THEN
 linkedCount ← linkedCount + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 coveragePercentage ← (linkedCount / totalRequirements) * 100
 
 OUTPUT "Requirements Coverage: " + coveragePercentage + "%"
 OUTPUT "Linked: " + linkedCount + "/" + totalRequirements
 OUTPUT "Uncovered: " + (totalRequirements - linkedCount) + " requirements"
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how to calculate requirements traceability coverage. It iterates through all requirements and checks if each has at least one link to downstream artifacts (design, code, or tests). The coverage percentage indicates how many requirements can be traced through the development lifecycle.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × m) where n = number of requirements, m = average number of artifacts per requirement

examples.title: Use Case Flow Validation
examples.description: Algorithm to validate use case flows ensuring proper structure and completeness
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT: useCase
 errors ← []
 warnings ← []
 
 // Check required elements exist
 IF useCase.name IS EMPTY THEN
 errors.add("Use case must have a name")
 END IF
 
 IF useCase.preconditions IS EMPTY THEN
 warnings.add("Consider adding preconditions")
 END IF
 
 IF useCase.postconditions IS EMPTY THEN
 errors.add("Use case must have postconditions")
 END IF
 
 IF useCase.basicFlow IS EMPTY THEN
 errors.add("Basic flow is required")
 END IF
 
 // Validate flow connections
 lastStepBasic ← useCase.basicFlow[LENGTH-1]
 IF lastStepBasic.postcondition ≠ useCase.postcondition THEN
 warnings.add("Basic flow should lead to postcondition")
 END IF
 
 // Check alternative flows reference valid basic flow steps
 FOR EACH altFlow IN useCase.alternativeFlows DO
 IF altFlow.refStep NOT IN useCase.basicFlow THEN
 errors.add("Alt flow '" + altFlow.name + "' references invalid step")
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 OUTPUT errors
 OUTPUT warnings
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm validates use case structure by checking for required elements (name, basic flow, postconditions), ensuring flows are properly connected, and verifying that alternative flows correctly reference basic flow steps. Such validation helps ensure use cases are complete and well-structured.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n + m) where n = basic flow steps, m = number of alternative flows
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k = number of errors/warnings stored

examples.title: Multiplicity Validation in Class Relationships
examples.description: Algorithm to validate multiplicities in class diagram relationships ensure consistency
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT: classA, classB, relationship, multiplicityA, multiplicityB
 validationResult ← PASS
 
 // Parse multiplicities
 minA, maxA ← PARSE(multiplicityA)
 minB, maxB ← PARSE(multiplicityB)
 
 // Validate relationship type compatibility
 IF relationship.type = "composition" THEN
 // Composition requires exactly one on 'whole' side
 IF maxA ≠ 1 AND relationship.wholeSide = "A" THEN
 validationResult ← FAIL
 OUTPUT "Composition requires multiplicity '1' on whole side"
 END IF
 IF minA ≠ 1 AND relationship.wholeSide = "A" THEN
 validationResult ← FAIL
 OUTPUT "Composition suggests minimum '1' on whole side"
 END IF
 END IF
 
 // Validate logical consistency
 IF relationship.type = "association" THEN
 // Check for reasonable ranges
 IF maxA = 0 OR maxB = 0 THEN
 validationResult ← FAIL
 OUTPUT "Multiplicity cannot be zero for active relationship"
 END IF
 END IF
 
 OUTPUT validationResult
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode validates that multiplicities assigned to class relationships are logically consistent with relationship types. For composition, it enforces that the 'whole' side has multiplicity of exactly 1, reflecting that parts cannot exist independently. This helps catch modeling errors early in the analysis phase.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) constant time parsing and validation
